key_insight: Modularity only helps continual learning when representations are lower-dimensional and structured; in high-dimensional lazy regimes, modular and single-network models behave similarly.
engineering_relevance: For sequential engineering AI systems, the benefit of task-specific modules depends on representational regime, so architectural modularity should be matched to initialization and capacity, not assumed universally beneficial.
method: Compared task-partitioned recurrent network vs single-network baseline
Induced high- and low-dimensional regimes by weight-scale manipulation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that both models perform similarly in the high-dimensional regime, while in the lower-dimensional regime the modular network learns task-specific subspaces that better separate or share structure according to task similarity.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on the specific initialization-scale-induced regime and sequential task order, so gains from modularity may not transfer without careful tuning of representation dimensionality.
paper_title: Dimensionality Controls When Modularity Helps in Continual Learning
